summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

A company's break-even point is where P(x) = 3x − 150 crosses the x-axis. What is the x-intercept?

Set P(x) = 0: 3x − 150 = 0, so x = 50 units for break-even.

A store's revenue is R(p) = −5p² + 200p − 500 dollars. What is the y-intercept and what does it mean?

R(0) = −5(0) + 200(0) − 500 = −500. If the product is free, the store loses $500 in costs.

A suspension cable follows y = 0.004x² − 2x + 300. What is the y-intercept, representing the cable height at the left tower?

The y-intercept is y(0) = 0.004(0) − 2(0) + 300 = 300 feet.
